questions.question: What is the time complexity of Merge Sort?
questions.explanation: Merge Sort is a divide-and-conquer algorithm with a time complexity of O(n log n) on average.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the time complexity of Quick Sort?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of Quick Sort is O(n log n) on average, but it can be O(n^2) in the worst-case scenario.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Left-Root-Right tree traversal in a Binary Tree?
options: To find the maximum value in the tree
To find the minimum value in the tree
To traverse the tree from left to right
To find the height of the tree
questions.explanation: The Left-Root-Right tree traversal is used to traverse a Binary Tree from left to right.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of using Strassen's Matrix Multiplication algorithm?
options: It has a time complexity of O(n^2.81)
It can only be used for square matrices
It is more efficient than standard matrix multiplication
It can only be used for 2x2 matrices
questions.explanation: Strassen's Matrix Multiplication algorithm has a time complexity of O(n^2.81), making it more efficient than standard matrix multiplication for large matrices.

questions.question: What is a challenge in multiplying two large integers using the standard multiplication algorithm?
questions.explanation: One of the challenges in multiplying two large integers using the standard multiplication algorithm is carry propagation, which can lead to errors and instability.
